Name: curvature_aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing via Deterministic Optimization Proxies
Short Hypothesis: Precision routing in neural networks can be optimized by leveraging curvature-aware diagnostics and deterministic optimization proxies, enabling formalized stability radii and explicit condition number constraints without training.
Related Work: Existing methods for precision routing often rely on heuristic control mechanisms or costly calibration, as seen in Spectral Quantization Routing and Spectral Latent Steering. Our proposal distinguishes itself by formalizing stability radii and leveraging deterministic spectral estimators for precise diagnostics, replacing heuristic fragility claims with explicit mathematical formulations.
Abstract: We propose a novel framework for precision routing in neural networks that leverages curvature-aware diagnostics and deterministic optimization proxies. By formalizing stability radii and explicit condition number constraints, our method enables precise precision allocation without training. We introduce deterministic spectral estimators to quantify gradient covariance decay, Hessian conditioning, and feature entanglement, operationalizing a training-free routing protocol. This approach replaces heuristic fragility claims with formalized stability radii, PAC-Bayes generalization bounds, and explicit condition number constraints under precision switching. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across WILDS benchmarks, our framework achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to existing methods. By anchoring precision routing to architectural theory rather than systems engineering, we guarantee resource efficiency and mathematical transparency, ensuring each iteration yields theoretically grounded routing strategies optimized for immediate empirical verification.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Validate the precision routing protocol on single-GPU setups using WILDS benchmarks. Measure throughput and accuracy to establish the Pareto frontier.
Experiment 2: Quantify gradient covariance decay, Hessian conditioning, and feature entanglement using deterministic spectral estimators. Compare these metrics with heuristic baselines.
Experiment 3: Evaluate the impact of formalized stability radii on model robustness under precision switching. Measure generalization stability using PAC-Bayes bounds.
Experiment 4: Conduct systematic ablation studies to isolate the contribution of curvature-aware diagnostics and deterministic optimization proxies to overall performance.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risk of increased computational overhead due to deterministic spectral estimators.
Limitations in scalability to multi-GPU setups without further optimization.
Dependence on accurate condition number constraints for effective precision routing.
